steps.title: Find the initial constant C
steps.content:
At the initial measurement, $t = 0$ and $P = 8000$. Substituting into $P = C(2)^{rt}$:
$$8000 = C(2)^{r \cdot 0} = C(2)^0$$
Since $(2)^0 = 1$:
$$8000 = C \cdot 1 = C$$
Therefore $C = 8000$.

steps.title: Substitute the 8-hour measurement and solve for r
steps.content:
At $t = 8$ hours, $P = 16000$. Substitute into the formula:
$$16000 = 8000(2)^{8r}$$
Divide both sides by 8000:
$$2 = (2)^{8r}$$
Rewrite $2$ as $2^1$:
$$2^1 = (2)^{8r}$$
Since the bases are equal, equate the exponents:
$$1 = 8r$$
$$r = \frac{1}{8}$$
This matches - B is correct.
